findings_applied.description: Removed blanket parallelism claim from Overview paragraph 1. Replaced 'All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput.' with 'Ingestion and transformation stages operate sequentially; delivery begins only after transformation is complete.' Eliminates direct contradiction with Stage 2 sequencing requirement.

Added a 'Security Considerations' section covering authentication, TLS in transit, encryption at rest, and access control. Updated Stage 4 cross-reference from 'Section 6' to 'the Security Considerations section below' to resolve the dangling reference.

Corrected inverted batch/streaming performance claim. Changed 'batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB. For larger payloads, streaming is preferred.' to 'streaming is 3× faster than batch for files under 10MB. For larger payloads, batch processing is preferred.' Also added 'Internal' qualifier before 'benchmarks' for traceability.

Removed unsupported 99.9% latency reduction claim. Changed 'reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes' to 'significantly reduces end-to-end write latency compared to unbuffered writes' per suggestion.
Defined the retry policy inline. Changed 'standard retry policy' to 'an exponential backoff retry policy (up to 3 attempts, doubling delay on each attempt)'.
Defined 'large files' threshold. Changed 'Large files are handled separately' to 'Files exceeding 100MB are handled separately'.

Standardized terminology to 'pipeline' throughout. Changed Operational Notes bullet 2 from 'The pipeline and workflow are monitored' to 'The pipeline is monitored'. Changed Conclusions from 'real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline' to 'real-time streaming capability alongside the existing batch pipeline' to eliminate mixed usage.
Expanded ETL acronym on first use. Changed 'an internal ETL system' to 'an internal ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) system'.

Specified authentication mechanism. Changed 'Authentication is used to verify each request' to 'API key authentication is used to verify each request'. Consistent with Stage 1 context (HTTP POST from SDK agents) and the Security Considerations section added for F-002.

notes: All 9 findings applied. Score recovery: 15 (F-001 critical) + 24 (F-002/003/004 high, 3×8) + 9 (F-005/006/007 medium, 3×3) + 2 (F-008/009 low, 2×1) = 50 points recovered. Estimated score: 47 + 50 = 97. F-002 resolution chose to add the missing Security Considerations section rather than remove the reference — the section is substantively warranted for an architecture brief and the reference implies the intent to include it. F-009 specified API key authentication as the most contextually consistent mechanism given the HTTP POST / SDK agent ingestion pattern; this could reasonably be marked TBD if a different mechanism is intended.
